old_claim.note: Factory System provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Industrial design is defined around the professional design of mass-produced consumer products and manufacturable goods.

source_shape.source_locator: Introduction defines industrial design as the design of mass-produced consumer products and says industrial designers help produce manufactured items.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source defines the field through mass-produced consumer products and manufactured items rather than through factory buildings alone.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports replacing factory_system with mass_production because the professional design scope is about the products being manufactured at scale, not merely production under one roof.

ontology_before: Industrial Design Profession depended directly on Factory System, tying the profession to mill organization instead of the mass-produced product scope.
ontology_after: Industrial Design Profession depends on Mass Production as the source-defined product and manufacturability context for industrial design.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: industrial_design_profession now depends on mass_production and no longer directly depends on factory_system.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to factory management or mill architecture rather than professional product design.
A source showed industrial design as a profession can be fully defined without mass-produced or manufactured products.
The mass_production node were narrowed away from standardized manufactured products.
short_reason: Industrial design is defined around mass-produced manufactured products.
